Notices Please check the home page for any weather - related information. Motor vehicle supplemental bills will be due on January 1, 2026, and the last day to pay will be February 2, 2026. Bills have been mailed and are available online. Partial payments can be made in the office with a check or by calling the Call Center. Maximum payment online or at the Call Center is $70, 000. To make payments over the phone, please call the Call Center number at 888 - 985 - 9908; fees apply for this service . The Call Center ID number for Brookfield is 54260. Autopay is no longer available . Delinquent motor vehicle bills from the Grand List 2022 have been sent to TaxServ Collection Agency. Please pay your motor vehicle taxes to avoid additional fees. Payments Paying By Mail : Mailing Address: Tax Collector - Brookfield P.O. Box 508 Brookfield, CT 06804 We will use the U.S. Postal Postmark to verify if the payment has been made on time . The US Postmark Rule Change could impact your postmark effective 12/24/2025. If you are mailing at the end of the payment cycle please go inside the Post Office for a postmark to insure your payment is on time. State law requires us to look at the US Postmark. If you would like a receipt for your payment, please send a self - addressed stamped envelope with your payment. You can also print out a receipt of your payment by viewing the website. The report will show the date the payment was processed. Paying in Person We accept up to $500 in cash per visit per day. We also accept bank checks, money orders, and personal checks. Paying Online Payments can also be made through our website using a credit / debit card for a fee of 3% (minimum fee of $2.00) or using your checking account via the e - check feature for $1.00. Accepted payment methods include: Visa, Mastercard, American Express, and Discover. Make a payment through our website . If you use your own bank's online bill pay services, please make sure you send the payment at the beginning of the collection. Often it takes many days or even weeks for it to arrive, and generally they do not come with a U.S. Postal Service postmark. If it is received after the due date, it will be considered late. Paying By Call Center (Provider ID # 54260) If you pay by phone, be prepared with your tax bill in your hand. Please provide the representative with the Provider ID # 54260. The current year is based on the grand list year. For real estate and personal property, you will use the list number. For motor vehicle bills, you will need to use the bill number found on the left side, middle section of the bill. If your list number is 00 you will enter 03, if it is 09 you will input 04. There can be multiple cars on a motor vehicle bill. You need to enter each one. An example of the current year bills Real Estate 2024 - 01 - 0008099 Personal Property 2024 - 02 - 0012100 Motor Vehicle 2024 - 03 - 0062222 Motor Vehicle 2024 - 04 - 0040951 Payment Returned If any payment is returned, there will be a fee of $20 added to your bill. If you have any questions, you can email the Tax Collector or by phone 203 - 775 - 7304. Collection Agency Delinquent motor vehicle bills from the 2021 Grand List and older (2009 - 2022GL) are with our collection agency, Tax Serv Capital Services LLC. Please make your payments to them by calling 860 - 724 - 9100. Motor Vehicles No Longer Owned If you have received a Motor Vehicle bill for a motor vehicle you no longer own, please contact the Assessor's Office to find out what you need to do to remove the bill or have it prorated for the correct time period. You can call the Assessor's Office at 203 - 775 - 7302 or email the Assessor's Office . We are here to assist you, so please don't hesitate to contact us. 2025 Tax Information View or pay your tax bills online, choose All Online Tax Payments and Payment History, insert your last name followed by a space and your first initial (do not use punctuation). The name must match your tax bill. Choose the IRS Payment Records for 2025 Option under the search bar, then click on Search. On the next screen, you will see the bills. There is a blue colored bar above the bills where you can click on the box next to IRS. Once you click on it, another button will appear above it that says View IRS. Click on it and you will get a report of payments made in 2025. Failure to receive a tax bill does not invalidate the tax or the interest due. As owners of property, taxpayers are responsible for knowing when their taxes are due. Note : Failure to receive a bill does not exempt the taxpayer from payment of all taxes and all interest charges, and collection costs, per Conn. Gen. Stat. 12 - 130 and 12 - 146. Interest is charged on all late payments. There are no exceptions to this policy. The Tax Collector's office is responsible for the collection of property taxes and water line assessments. The office operates under the authority of the Connecticut General Statutes and Brookfield Town Ordinances. Box 508 Brookfield, CT 06804 Phone: 203 - 775 - 7304 Fax: 203 - 775 - 5317 Hours of Operation Monday through Wednesday 8 am to 4 pm Thursday 8 am to 6 pm Friday 8 am to 1 pm Directory FAQs What do I own that is subject to taxes? Three types of property are assessed and subject to taxes: Real Estate, Motor Vehicle, and Business Personal Property. Any land or buildings you own are considered real estate. Registered motorized or unmotorized vehicles (including cars, trucks, and motorcycles) are considered motor vehicles for tax purposes. Business Personal property is a general category including business equipment, machinery, furniture, and fixtures either owned or leased by the business. Unregistered motor vehicles are also taxed as personal property. I need to register my car. What do I do? If you owe delinquent property taxes on any vehicle in your name, you may not renew any registrations at the Department of Motor Vehicles without paying your taxes first. All past due taxes in your name must be paid in full for an immediate clearance. Clearances are now applied directly through the DMV website, so stamps are no longer utilized. It will take 24 to 72 hours for the clearance to be processed through the DMV system. What is the best way to pay my tax bill? The most convenient way is by mail, in person at the tax office, or online with a credit card or e - check. To pay by credit card or e - check, visit the Pay Taxes Online / Tax Inquiry page . Credit / Debit Cards are now accepted as a form of payment in the Tax Office. There are fees for this service. If paying by mail, please send us the appropriate payment coupon (A or B) with your check. If you wish to have a receipt returned to you, please send the entire tax bill and a self - addressed, stamped envelope with your payment. You will receive a receipt back within a few days. We will not send back your receipt if you fail to include a self - addressed, stamped envelope. The 2024 Grand List mill rate is 23.28. Use the Assessed Value to Calculate. Assessed Value is 70% of the Appraised Value. This is for informational purposes only. If you have questions, please feel free to contact the Assessor's Office at 860 - 779 - 3411, option 6. Welcome to Your Assessor's Office! The primary function of the Assessor's Office is to prepare the Grand List each year. The Grand List is comprised of three parts: Real Estate, Personal Property, and Motor Vehicles. The Assessor's Office must discover, list, and equitably value all taxable and exempt property located within the Town of Brooklyn in accordance with Connecticut General Statutes. This office is also responsible for administering exemption and abatement programs. Note : The office is open to the public. Our assessment data can also be accessed online. FAQs What if I no longer live in Brooklyn, but still live in Connecticut and I receive a motor vehicle tax bill from Brooklyn? You pay the July 2017 motor vehicle tax bill to the town you were residing in on October 1, 2016. If you did not change your address with the DMV, please do so as soon as possible. The DMV sends your motor vehicle listing to the town that is on the address of your registration, as of October 1, 2016. If the address is wrong, it will go to the wrong town and have to be transferred. We do not pro - rate between towns. What if I got a new vehicle and used the same plate? If you took the plate from one vehicle and put it on another vehicle, you do not have to do anything. The billing will follow that plate. If you did this after October 1, 2016, the bill that you receive in July 2017 will be on the old motor vehicle, and next January you will receive a pro - rated tax bill on the new motor vehicle, with a pro - rated credit from the old motor vehicle applied to the new motor vehicle. How is the value of my property arrived at? Personal Property, other than registered motor vehicles, is assessed at 70% of its value, as determined by use of straight - line depreciation, as of October 1, 2016. Real Property is assessed at 70% of its value as of October 1, 2015, the date of the last revaluation. Registered motor vehicles are required to be assessed at 70% of the average retail value, using the October edition of the NADA cost guides. Whether you are a visitor or a resident, we have much to offer! Brooklyn lies in the center of Windham County in the northeastern corner of Connecticut. There are approximately sixty miles of road, with seventeen miles of it along scenic byway route 169. Take a tour through the historic center of Town, where you will find the old courthouse, now housing Town hall offices, monuments recognizing men and women who served in famous battles, old standing churches, and much more. We have a vibrant commercial district where you can eat, shop, and sleep. Looking for some quiet outdoor family activities? Visit one of our four park locations where you can hike many trails, launch your kayak into the Quinebaug River, play a game of disc golf or volleyball, or stroll through the wildflowers on land preserved by our Conservation Commission. Brooklyn is a growing town with a hearty farm industry. We are the home to the oldest agricultural fair in Connecticut. We offer an excellent Pre - K through 8th - grade school system and a very active and always growing Recreation Department. The Town of Brooklyn is governed using a Town Meeting - style of government with a three - member elected Board of Selectmen and an elected Board of Finance. The First Selectman is the Chief Executive Officer of the Town and is an ex officio member, without a vote, of all Boards and Commissions in the Town. He is responsible for the Town's day - to - day operations and oversees all of the Town departments. Welcome to the Town of Brooklyn. We will strive to do the best possible for our Town. My door is open to all citizens. Members are appointed by the Board of Selectmen for four - year terms. Responsibilities of the Commission include the following: Preparation of Brooklyn’s Plan of Conservation and Development every ten years and any amendments as needed; Preparation and update of Brooklyn’s Subdivision Regulations; Review and approval/denial of all applications for subdivision or resubdivision of land; Preparation and update of Brooklyn’s Zoning Regulations, which creates separate zones within the town and establishes standards of land use within those zones; and The review and approval/denial of various zoning applications. The Brooklyn Planning and Zoning Commission holds regular meetings on the first Wednesday of every month. See Regular Meeting Schedule for modified frequency. The Town Planner provides technical assistance to the Commission. Find forms relating to Planning and Zoning applications. Search 2024 Grand List (XLSX) Application for a Motor Vehicle Property Tax Exemption - Active Military - Resident (PDF) Assessment Appeals Assessor Field Card Assessor's Tax Maps Map 1 to 25 Map 26 to 50 Map 51 to 73 Board of Assessment Appeals Farm, Forest & Open Space Forms & Applications GIS How Assessments Are Derived Income & Expense Brochure & FAQs (PDF) Mill Rate History (PDF) Motor Vehicle Information Personal Property - Affidavit of Business Closed (PDF) Tax Relief Programs for the Elderly & Disabled Tax Relief Programs for Veterans Veterans' Exemptions Brochure (PDF) Taxpayer Information Requests Home Departments Assessor Assessor Understanding the Budget Process 2026 - 02 - 12 Understanding the Budget Process Presentation Responsibilities Our office is responsible for the annual preparation of the Grand List. The Grand List is a compilation of all taxable property within the town limits - including motor vehicles, personal property and real estate. Real Estate Our records are updated from deeds and documents recorded with the Town Clerk, updates from the Building and Land Use Departments, and other state, regional, and local entities. We update the Tax Maps annually, in conjunction with the completion of the Grand List. Properties that have building permits that have not yet been closed out by the Assessor's Office are visited annually with the property record being updated with new information. Additionally, any properties that are changed or updated without permits are also visited and updates made to the assessment records. Haddam had 4, 292 real estate accounts on the 2018 Grand List. Motor Vehicles The Assessor's Office receives and updates motor vehicle records from the Connecticut Department of Motor Vehicles. We are supplied lists from the DMV twice per year and are responsible for valuing and maintaining accurate listings of vehicles within Haddam - including adding and removing motor vehicles from out - of - state or other jurisdictions, deleting motor vehicles that were sold or traded - in and adding any new vehicles that were registered after October 1st. Personal Property The Assessor's Office maintains personal property records for all businesses and personal property accounts in town, and this includes the annual processing of Personal Property Declarations. We are responsible for maintaining accurate records for businesses moving into and out of Haddam and recording the accurate personal property listing of these businesses. This also includes taxpayers who have unregistered motor vehicles, or vehicles registered out of state, whose principal domicile is here in Haddam. Exemptions & Special Programs In addition to maintaining the Grand List, the Assessor's Office maintains records for a multitude of state and local exemption programs - from initial eligibility and application to the annual maintenance of these records. Some of these include exemptions for the Blind, Veterans, Active Military, Elderly, Manufacturing, Solar, and Farm and Forest, among others. In conjunction with maintaining these programs in Haddam, we are responsible for reporting on these programs to various state and local authorities throughout the year. Please contact us directly with any questions. 1 72 Maple Ave 2 117 Jacoby 3 114 Walkley Hill Rd 4 14 Landing Rd South 5 12 Young St Contact Us Tammy Anderson (CCMA I) Assessor Email Phone: 860 - 345 - 8531 Ext. 226 Chelsea Cleveland Assessor Technician Email Chelsea Cleveland Phone: 860 - 345 - 8531 Ext. 213 Frequently Asked Questions Did you sell or dispose of a motor vehicle are you going to sell or dispose of a motor vehicle? If the answer is yes you must return or cancel your registration plates with the Department of Motor Vehicle (DMV) and obtain a plate receipt that you will need to provide to the Assessor's Office along with proof of what happened to that Vehicle. This will allow us to either remove or prorate the Motor Vehicle. The bill you receive in July will be from October 1st to the month of disposal. View the Motor Vehicle - Standard Forms (PDF) regarding the proof of accepted documents and the time frame adjustment period required by the Connecticut State Statutes. If you sold or disposed of a Motor Vehicle but transferred the plates to a new vehicle then it will be reflected in the billing. For example : You owned a 2010 Ford F150 on October 1, 2018, and sold that vehicle on December 21, 2018, and transferred the plates to a 2014 Chevy Silverado. You will get a bill for the 2010 F150 in July of 2019. The plate transfer and subsequent 2014 Chevy will be accounted for on the 2018 Supplemental List, and you will receive a supplemental bill in January 2020. This supplemental bill will list the credit received for the 2010 Ford F150 and will give you the net difference in the taxes owed between the two vehicles. This can be quite confusing at times, especially if you transferred a plate to more than 1 vehicle in the grand list year, which will give you a credit for what you paid on the 2010 vehicle towards the 2014 vehicle. Call our office if you have any questions or need any clarification on your particular situation. The U.S. Postal Service recently put out a warning that mail may not be postmarked on the same day it is dropped off. A new USPS rule clarifies that postmarks are applied at regional processing centers, not local post offices, and may reflect a later date. What you can do: If you need a same - day postmark, take your mail to the post office counter and request a manual postmark. Mailing items early is strongly recommended. Ways to Make a Payment Mail payments in the self - addressed envelope that came with your bills. Payments must be postmarked by the Post Office before or on August 1, 2025. Make a payment online. If you are paying online, please visit the Before Paying Online tab . Stop in the office during Town Office hours. We accept Cash, Check, Money Order or Credit/Debit Card. A secure tax drop box is located outside the front door for check payments made after hours. If you would like a receipt, include a self - addressed stamped envelope with your payment. 2024 Grand List Tax Payment Reminder 2 nd Installment of Real Estate, Personal Property and all Supplemental Motor Vehicle tax bills. Due January 1, 2026 and accepted without interest if paid on or before February 2, 2026. The last day for postmarking is February 2, 2026. If mailed this day, make sure to take the envelope into the Post Office for postmarking. 2024 Grand List Information: The Mil Rate for the 2024 Grand List is 32.46 for Motor Vehicle Bills and 34.35 for Real Estate and Personal Property Bills. This covers the period from October 1, 2024, to September 30, 2025. Tax bills were mailed on June 30, 2025, for the 2024 Grand List. The first installment was due by August 1, 2025. If you have a question about a bill you received or didn't receive, please give us a call as soon as possible. We do not mail out bills in January for Real Estate or Personal Property. Please hold on to the payment stubs for the January installment. The second installment is due by February 2, 2026. If you had a change to your escrow account, bought or sold a house, please make sure you know who is responsible for the taxes. A quick phone call to your mortgage company can save you many hassles and interest charges. If you sold a car, please contact the Assessor's Office to remove it from the tax rolls. DMV does not notify the Town of Haddam. Search Absentee Ballot Application (PDF) Mail - in Voter Poll Worker Information Voter Registration Information Voting in Connecticut Home Departments Registrar of Voters Registrar of Voters The Registrars of Voters are elected officials of Haddam. The office is governed by General Statues and financially supported by the municipality. The Registrar of Voters, as the chief election officials, have a wide variety of responsibilities from preparing and certifying the official voting list, to ensuring we have fair, accurate and efficient elections. The staff attends meetings regularly called by the Secretary of State to keep current on new and pending legislation as it is proposed and passed. In addition to overseeing the elections, primaries, and referenda, they maintain the voter registration files, register voters, and conduct an annual canvass of voters. The Registrars are also responsible for making changes to voter lists based on information received from various sources, such as the DMV. They also remove those who have been convicted of a felony or have moved out of town. The Registrars also process all local and statewide petitions. Registrars work with various organizations to maintain voter registration at the highest possible level. Voter registration drives are conducted at the local high school, senior centers, as well as special functions. Election, Primaries or Referenda time is an extremely busy period for the office, as they are responsible for preparing the budget for all elections, conducting the lottery for municipal candidates, making all the arrangements for the three polling locations in Haddam, and the hiring and training of approximately 30 poll workers and election officials. Just days before a voting session, the office must prepare an official voter list of active and inactive voters for the three polling places, arrange for the set - up and removal of all voting equipment before and immediately after an election, and inspect and pre - test all voting machines. On the day of the election, the Registrars must supervise and assist all election officials in addition to voter inquiries. After the election is completed, they ultimately submit the election results to the Secretary of the State and the Town Clerk. Election audits are held at the discretion of the Secretary of State to help ensure the accuracy of voting machines. Search CT Statutes on Taxation - Local Levy DMV Informational Links Legal Notices Lookup & Pay Tax Bill Mill Rate History Tax Refund Form Instructions Tax Sale Information (PDF) Home Departments Tax Collector Tax Collector General Tax Bill Information Tax bills are mailed out only once a year in the middle of June. It is the responsibility of the taxpayer to remember to pay the second installment in January. All bills can be found on the town website, look for the "Pay Tax Bills" button on the home page. Search by last name, with a space and then the initial of your first name. Real Estate & Personal Property Bills All real or personal property tax bills over $100 are payable in 2 installments. The first installment is due July 1 and the last day to pay without penalty is August 1. Second installments are due January 1, and the last day to pay is February 1. There are three pieces to the Bill for Real Estate and Personal Property: Part A is for the July Installment, Part B for the January Installment and Part C is the taxpayer's copy. Delinquent interest on taxes due accrues as of the due date with interest charged monthly at an interest rate of 1.5% of the unpaid taxes with a $2 minimum charge. If you escrow money each month with your lender, we send the bill to the lender. If you escrow and receive a bill, please forward the bill to your bank immediately. If you no longer escrow your taxes and you did not receive a bill, please contact immediately at. Failure to receive a tax bill does not invalidate the tax. Connecticut General State Section 12 - 130 states that the failure to receive a tax bill does not relieve the taxpayer of the requirement to pay the bill. Consequently, if you do not receive a tax bill(s); the tax bill is due with any interest, fees and charges that might have accrued. If you did not receive a tax bill, call the Tax Collector's Office. Motor Vehicle Tax Bills Motor Vehicle taxes are due and payable in full July 1 and the last day to pay is August 1. Bills are mailed in the middle of June. Motor Vehicle Supplement taxes (registrations after October 1 st of any given year) are due in full January 1. Bills are mailed in December. Visit the Connecticut DMV Delinquent Motor Vehicle Bills Are Reported to DMV Payment Options By Mail From July 1 to August 1 and January 1 to February 1, please use the yellow envelopes provided with the bill. These go to a Webster Bank processing center in Boston Ma. At all other times mail to: Town of Old Saybrook 302 Main St Old Saybrook, CT 06475 Atten: Tax Department Payments postmarked on or before August 1, 2025 will be considered as on - time payments. Have your envelop hand - canceled at the U.S. Post Office. Electronic bill - pay services processed using your personal bank arrive bearing no postmark. Please keep in mind, if there is no postmark, timeliness depends solely upon our receipt of the payment. Online Tax Bill Payment All bills can be found on the town website, look for the "Pay Tax Bills" button on the home page. Search by last name, with a space and then the initial of your first name. Fees charged for online service: $0.95 for paying by e - check $3.95 flat fee for paying by debit card (Visa or MasterCard) 2.5% of your total bill when paying by credit card ($2 min) In Person Payment Payments can be made in person at the Town Hall 1 st Floor, Tax Collector, 302 Main St, Old Saybrook. Parking is limited, we encourage paying by mail or online. Hours of operation: Monday through Friday, 8:30 am thru 4:30 pm, except Holidays. We accept Checks, Money Orders, Cash (close to the exact amount). If you are having trouble paying your tax bill online, please contact the tax department. TaxServ Capital Services Please be advised The Town of Old Saybrook is under contract with TaxServ Capital Services for the collection of the Town delinquent Personal Property taxes. If you receive a letter from TaxServ, you must call TaxServ to discuss your account and arrange payment. If you do call the Towns tax or assessor's office, you will be directed to TaxServ. You will be receiving phone calls and letters from: TaxServ Capital Services, LLC 25 Prescott Street, 2 nd Floor West Hartford, CT 06110 - 2334 Telephone 860 - 724 - 9100 Email taxserv@taxserv.com Erin Colwell Tax Collector Phone 860 - 395 - 3138 Address Town Hall Tax Collector's Office 302 Main Street Old Saybrook 06475 United States Hours Monday - Friday 8:30 - 4:30 Information General Tax Questions (PDF) FAQs How Is the Tax (Mill) Rate Established? Search How to File a Building Permit Online Online Permit Applications Home Departments Building Building Please visit the Building Department to review property files. We do not relay property file contents electronically or via telephone. Welcome to the Old Saybrook Building Department For general information, please contact us via email . The Building Department is responsible for monitoring all commercial and residential building activities through the issuance of permits and inspections. This includes new construction, renovated buildings, electrical, plumbing, heating, air conditioning, demolitions, gas and oil tank installations/removals, and more. Do I Need a Building Permit? (PDF) Old Saybrook Building Department is regulated by State Statutes. A close working relationship is maintained with the town's Land Use Department and Fire Marshal . The Building Department reviews building permit applications and site plans, directs the applicant to other agencies, answers questions on the building process, and works to protect the town's health, safety, and welfare. Once the building permit application and all associated documents have been approved, the Building Official ( Tom Makowicki ) visits active work sites, reviewing work and approving various stages of construction. Final inspections signal the end of the review process, with new or renovated buildings receiving a formal certificate of occupancy, which allows the structure to become occupied. Any plans for development should be reviewed by the Building Department for adherence to regulatory statutes. Note : It is the homeowner's responsibility to be sure their contractors have permits. Connecticut State Statutes have penalties for violations, including fines up to $1, 000 and possible imprisonment. Documentation required for permitting includes (but is not limited to): Completed ONLINE Application for Building Permit Contractor's Current License Contractor's Current Certificate of Liability Insurance/Workers' Comp. Permit Fee Payment Permit Fee Schedule (PDF) Please note that additional permitting requirements must be met for homes located in the Flood Zone. Applications will not be reviewed until all required documents and fees are submitted. Quick Facts Fences 7 feet or less in height do not require a building permit. Can go right to the property line. Sheds 10 feet by 12 feet or smaller (less than 120 square feet) do not require a building permit. Must speak with the Land Use Department regarding placement. Driveways do not require a building permit. If the driveway will encroach on town property, please see the DPW/First Selectman's office for a Driveway Encroachment Permit. Tents 20 feet by 40 feet or smaller (less than 900 square feet) and with a capacity of less than 50 people do not require a building permit. Septic : Septic information within the Building Files is limited. Please contact CRAHD 860 - 661 - 3300 . For pump - out status, please contact the septic company last used to pump out your tank. Fenwick : If your property is within Fenwick Please Call: 860 - 227 - 5399. Fenwick has its own Building Department. Property line disputes /issues with neighbors are civil matters and not addressed by the Building Department. Please visit the Building Department to review property files. We do not relay property file contents electronically or via telephone. title: Never ignore a tax bill. Taxes will remain due and interest will continue to accumulate until the bill is paid or removed by the Assessor. Failure to receive a tax bill does not exempt the requirement that you pay taxes and/or interest. You are responsible for obtaining your tax bill and paying your taxes on time. Interest accumulates at 1.5% per month or partial month taxes are overdue. ALL Taxes are formally due on 1 July with 2nd payment deferments (50% of due tax) for Real Estate, Personal Property due 1 January. No additional notice will be mailed if you choose to defer these taxes. ALL Motor Vehicles Taxes are due 1 July and do not have deferments. Motor Vehicles Changes (i.e. Buy, sell, re-register, transfer) will generate a Supplemental Motor Vehicle tax due on 1 January, you have a one month grace period to pay these taxes. If you are late you will pick up the grace month as part of your interest charges

Home Departments Assessor Assessor Duties & Responsibilities The Assessor’s Office annually provides a listing of assessment for each and every property within the town limits of Preston. The listing is referred to as the Grand List and provides the basis against which taxes will be levied during the following fiscal year. In addition, the Assessor’s Office maintains an up - to - date inventory of all property, including land and improvements thereon, including changes in ownership, new construction, alterations, and subdivisions filed as of the assessment date, and lists Connecticut registered motor vehicles and business personal property which are assessed for inclusion as part of the Grand List. Additionally, the Assessor and staff administer state and local programs of tax relief, apply exemptions as permitted by state law, and assist the public with a variety of inquiries. Current MILL Rate The current MILL Rate for Fiscal year 2024 - 2025 is 25.11. Real Estate Information The Assessor's Office is responsible for the appraisal of all Real Property located in the Town of Preston. Real Estate is assessed at 70% of the appraised value as established during the last Revaluation. Assessments will remain constant until the next Revaluation unless new construction, improvements or physical changes are discovered. Revaluation Information The Town of Preston contracted with Vision Government Solutions Inc. for the October 1, 2022 revaluation. The new values were implemented on October 1, 2022 and will stay in effect until September 30, 2027. Any new construction, additions, upgrades, etc. will be added to the new values. The next revaluation will be October 1, 2027. The Assessor’s Office maintains information on real estate ownership, sales, descriptions and values. Field cards and Maps are also available online at the Town of Preston Geographic and Property Information Network website . Tax Relief & Exemption Programs Questions concerning tax relief and exemption programs should be directed to the Assessor's Office. Please feel free to contact us during office hours at 860 - 887 - 5581, ext. 107. Billing Questions about billing, tax balances due, amounts paid and interest should be directed to the Tax Collector's Office at 860 - 887 - 5581, ext. 111. Home Departments Building Department Building Department Building Department Mission Statement: The Building Department is a professional office committed to preserving the health, welfare and safety of Preston residents, businesses and the general public. Our goal is to not only assist applicants, in the permit process, but also to provide thorough, consistent and efficient administration of the Connecticut State Building Code. We seek open communication and cooperation between all of the land use departments in order to better serve the community of Preston. We strive to provide a high quality of customer service with timely responsiveness, professionalism and continually find ways to improve our department. The primary function of the Building Department is to insure the health, safety and general welfare of the public. This is accomplished by: Performing plan reviews. Issuing permits and conducting inspections to insure compliance with the Connecticut State Building Code. Issuing necessary orders and notices to remove illegal or unsafe conditions. Interpreting and providing guidance regarding all applicable codes to Architects, Engineers, Contractors, Developers and other interested parties. Reviewing applications with the Town Planner, Zoning Enforcement Officer, Fire Marshal, Health Department, Sanitarian and Inland Wetlands Officer, along with various other local, state, and federal agencies as may be required. A permit is required whenever any owner or authorized agent intends to construct, enlarge, alter, repair, move, demolish, or change the occupancy of a building or structure, or to erect, install, enlarge, alter, repair, remove, convert or replace any electrical, gas, mechanical, or plumbing system. title: First Selectman
hours: Create a Website Account - Manage notification subscriptions, save form progress and more. Website Sign In Search Government Departments Services Community How Do I... Home Government Board of Selectmen Board of Selectmen Board of Selectmen Sandra L. Allyn - Gauthier, (D) First Selectwoman Term: November 18, 2025 to November 16, 2027 Gerald W. Grabarek, (D) Second Selectman Term: November 18, 2025 to November 16, 2027 Kenneth L. Zachem, (R) Third Selectman Term: November 18, 2025 to November 16, 2027 Nancy Smullen, Executive Assistant Office Hours Tuesday, Wednesday and Friday 9 a.m. - 4:30 p.m. Thursday 9 a.m. - 6:30 p.m. Holiday Schedule 2026 Town of Preston Holiday Schedule Overview The Board of Selectmen consists of three Selectmen. The First Selectman is the chief executive officer and ex - officio member of town boards, commissions and committees. The Second and Third Selectmen serve in an advisory capacity to the First Selectman, who assigns to them tasks and duties as required. The duties of the Board of Selectmen may be read in Chapter 91 of the Connecticut State Statutes. In addition, the CT State Statutes dictate that there cannot be more than two Selectman of the same political party. Notice of each meeting is filed with the Preston Town Clerk at minimum 24 hours prior to the meeting. Members of the public are welcomed to attend and to participate during a Public Comment period. Selectmen's Office The First Selectwoman leads and manages the business of the Selectmen's Office and oversees department managers and the operations of the town to ensure compliance with town ordinances and State Statutes, and coordinates with other elected officials. The office is responsible for developing strategic short and long - term initiatives for the relevant functional areas. In addition, she oversees Human Resources, IT, and the Resident State Troopers and serves as a primary point of contact for Economic Development. The First Selectwoman works closely with the Superintendent and the Finance Director to maximize resources and cost efficiencies through sharing of resources, budgeting, capital planning, and risk management and insurance programs. The Selectmen attend numerous meetings with boards, commissions, business owners, and citizens to maintain and improve town business and communication throughout all areas of the town. The First Selectwoman works closely with the State General Assembly members and State agencies as well as regional partners, groups, and non - profits (Council of Small Towns, Connecticut Conference of Municipalities, Southeastern Connecticut Council of Governments, and the Southeastern Connecticut Resources Recovery Authority, SeCTer, Uncas Health District and many others). The First Selectwoman and the internal team proactively seek grants to maximize opportunities while balancing the tax burden for the betterment of the community. The office maintains the town's website and Facebook page as part of its community engagement and communication efforts. Home Departments Planning & Zoning Department Planning & Zoning Department The Planning and Zoning Department strives to provide customer - oriented services and administration of the Zoning and Subdivision Regulations for the associated Land Use applications. The department oversees the preparation and coordination of both short and long range planning; and works in conjunction with all Land Use Commissions and Board for the successful completion of projects. Responsibility Our primary responsibility are: Reviews of Site Plans, Subdivision Plans, Special Exceptions and Amendments to both the Zoning and Subdivision Regulations. To update the Plan of Conservation & Development every 10 years. Coordinate all GIS mapping Town Planner The Town Planner is under the First Selectperson and is the advisor to the Planning and Zoning Commission (PZC). The planner reviews, comments, and provides reports on applications presented to the PZC; and will assist the other Land Use Board or Commissions upon their request. Zoning Enforcement Officer The Zoning Enforcement Officer (ZEO) is part of this department and administers all Zoning Permits, Zoning Complaints, and Variances. The ZEO is also the staff advisor to the Zoning Board of Appeals. Meetings The Planning and Zoning Commission meets on the 4th Tuesday of each month at 7:00 p.m. at the Preston Town Hall Lower Level Conference Room. The Zoning Board of Appeal’s meeting is held on the 2nd Monday of each month at 6 p.m. at the Preston Town Hall Lower Level Conference Room. Note, the rule explicitly warns that customer - applied pre - printed labels (e.g., from Self Service Kiosks, Click - N - Ship, or postage meters) only show when postage was printed, not when the USPS accepted the item. You are strongly encouraged to request a hand - canceled postmark at a USPS retail counter (even with Certified Mail) or use Registered Mail. When paying for postage at a USPS retail counter, the Postage Validation Imprint (PVI) label applied by the USPS also indicates the date of acceptance. Use of outdoor collection boxes on or near the due date is discouraged for municipal tax payments and any assessment filings that specify postmarks are accepted for meeting due dates. Town of Waterford's Affordable Housing Plan Click the image above to find out more about Waterford's Affordable Housing Plan As part of Public Act 17 - 170, the Connecticut Legislature established a requirement that every municipality in Connecticut prepare and adopt an "affordable housing plan" by June 1, 2022 and update the plan every five years thereafter (Section 8 - 30j of the Connecticut General Statutes). The legislation requires that the plan "shall specify how the municipality intends to increase the number of affordable housing developments in the municipality." The Planning and Zoning Commission led this project in partnership with consulting firm Planimetrics. The Waterford Affordable Housing Plan was adopted on May 10, 2022. Search Certificate of Trade Name Charter & Code of the Town of Wethersfield Connecticut Licensing Info Center DEEP CT Fishing Information DEEP CT Hunting & Trapping Information Dog Licenses Information For Dog Owners Chapter 70, Dogs & Other Animals Marriage License Requirements New Land Records Fees Home Departments Town Clerk A A Town Clerk The Town Clerk's office provides information regarding land records, including but not limited to deeds, mortgages, assignments, probate, and maps. The Town Clerk is the Registrar of Vital Statistics. Birth, marriage, and death records are kept by the Town Clerk's office. Military Discharges are also retained here. The office issues marriage licenses, dog licenses, sporting licenses, liquor permits, and trade name certificates. Absentee Ballots are issued in the Town Clerk's office for all state and local elections, primaries, and most referenda. The Town Clerk also acts as the Clerk of the Town Council, keeping agendas, minutes, and schedules of the meetings, and keeps current membership rosters of Town Boards and Commissions and Committees. Oaths of Office are administered to the Town Council members, as well as the Town's Justices of the Peace, Notaries Public, Constables, Police and Fire Department personnel, and some Board and Commission members, as required. Land Records The Wethersfield land records index is searchable online from 1970 to the present. While the index is available to view online, a subscription is required to view and print documents. Subscription fees are $125 for 1 month or $250 for 6 months. All land records can be accessed in the office. Copies of documents are $1 per page. Certified documents are $2 per document plus $1 per page. We are looking forward to implementing a more user - friendly, cost - effective system in the future. This will allow most people to obtain land record copies from their home or office going as far back as 1970. Absentee Ballots Absentee ballots are issued by the Town Clerk's office. Effective January 1, 2025, all absentee ballot applications must be titled "2025 Application for Absentee Ballot" and can be obtained in the Town Clerk's office, or from the Secretary of the State's website by using this link - CLICK HERE . This is a two - step process that requires completing and submitting an absentee ballot application. Once the application has been submitted and verified, a ballot can be issued. All absentee ballots have to be received by 8:00 pm on Election Day, in order for the vote to count. Cigarette Dealer Application Notice Notice of Cigarette Dealer/Retailer License applications that have been filed with the Connecticut Department of Revenue Services are posted below. (CGS 12 - 287). Once received, these notices are posted for a period of two weeks. Notary Services The Town Clerk's office provides notary services to the general public for a $5 fee each time the notary seal is used. Current photo identification is required. (Some documents, including Wills, Power of Attorney and Health Directives cannot be notarized by this office.) Notary Certification Filing The filing of Notary Appointment certificates from the Secretary of State's office is also done in the Town Clerk's office. The fee to file the Notary certificate is $20. The fee to file a Notary certificate name change or address change within town is $15, and to a new town is $10. Notary Authentication certificate is $5. Payment Options The Town Clerk's office accepts cash or check only. NO credit or debit cards are accepted at this time. Search Town Manager's Report Home Government Town Manager A A Town Manager About Us The Town Manager is responsible for the preparation of the proposed General Fund and Capital Improvement Budgets, controlling and directing all town agencies, coordinating the town's day - to - day operations, and overseeing the numerous responsibilities and duties associated with the Manager's position as Chief Executive Officer. Form of Government The Town of Wethersfield has a Council/Manager form of government. The Town Council sets policies and programs for the community. It consists of nine members who serve two - year terms. The Town Manager is a paid administrator who helps implement the policies set by the council. Town Budget and Audit Reports You may access the Audit Reports and Town Budgets for Wethersfield. Website Sign In About Departments Boards & Committees Help Center Search Attention Roofers Building Fees Building Permit Requirements for Workers' Compensation Construction Plan Requirements Schedule of Required Inspections Online Building and Land Use Permit System Home Departments Building Department Building Department All Building and Land Use applications are submitted online via OpenGov . Property Files and Information may be viewed online via AxisGIS To schedule an inspection, please call 860 - 487 - 3123 (leave a message) All applications must be applied for online. You can access the Town's application portal . Historic permits are also available via the "search" option. That system may be accessed via the link to the system to the left of this page or on the OpenGov website . If you are looking for a document that may be located within our property files, please visit the Town's GIS to view available property files or email a staff member. We will attempt to locate the record you are looking for and provide it to you electronically. If you have questions or would like to schedule an appointment or building inspection, please call the office at 860 - 487 - 3123. Do not email the Building Official directly. Please be advised that any structure intended for swimming or 'recreational bathing' that is capable of holding water 24" or more in depth is regulated by the Building Code and requires a permit before installation. This regulation includes storable pools (often in blue vinyl) of a temporary nature. The code requirements for a safety barrier for these pools are the same as for permanent above - ground or inground pools. Since the cost of the required safety barrier often exceeds the cost of the pool, it might be wise to speak with the Building Department before you purchase the pool. The Building Department can explain the code requirements and permit process. Contact the Building Department at 860 - 487 - 3123. When is a Building Permit Required? Any owner or authorized agent who intends to construct, enlarge, alter, repair, move, demolish or change the occupancy of a building or structure, or to erect, install, enlarge, alter, repair, remove, convert or replace any electrical, gas, mechanical or plumbing system, the installation of which is regulated by the building code, or to cause any such work to be done, shall first make application to the Building Official and obtain the required permit. Required Inspections This list of required inspections is meant to provide guidance and to augment the required inspections that are part of the building code. This does not limit the Building Official from requiring additional inspections as needed to ensure substantial compliance with the applicable codes and referenced standards. Forms and rebar, and excavation for footings and piers Rebar for foundation and retaining walls Footing drains Waterproofing Radon mitigation elements Vapor barrier All underground utilities, including plumbing, drainage, gas, and electrical, including the well Nailing for all sheathing and braced wall sections Air barrier before siding Complete rough including framing, electrical, plumbing, HVAC Electrical service, including conduit, before backfill Chimney throat inspections All fuel - fired appliances and venting, including pellet and wood stoves Sheetrock installation before taping when part of a braced wall section Ice and water shield Final inspection, which includes grading a minimum 6" pitch in 10' away from the structure For other than residential applications, additional items will require inspection. Some of those would be for site improvements, and others would be for elements not generally found in residential construction. These types of issues will be managed on a case - by - case basis, and we are happy to discuss with our contractors ahead of anticipated inspections.
